senses.gloss: to join, to couple, to yoke (بَيْنَ (bayna) "objects" or "accusative object" بِ (bi) "object")

examples.text: هٰذَا الْبَرْنَامَجُ يَقْرُنُ الْقَادِمِينَ الْجُدُدَ بِقَادَةِ الْأَعْمَالِ ذَوِي الْخِبْرَةِ.
examples.english: This program pairs newcomers with experienced business leaders.

senses.gloss: to join several strings, bind several animals or prisoners together to the same string

etymology.text: From Proto-Semitic *ḳarn- (“horn”). The root is associated with the sense of “being doubled, opposed, or equal”, and the meaning “century” can be derived from this through “equal in age, people of the same age”. Otherwise, it could have been borrowed from Ancient Greek χρόνος (khrónos, “time”, also “lifespan”).
